frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: Honestly, sometimes I feel bad when women talk about all men being potential threats. Like, I get it, safety is important, but not all guys are like that, yaar. When I see girls crossing the road or choosing women-only cabs, it's like they're automatically assuming the worst about *all* of us. It's a bit unfair to the good guys, no?
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This comment demonstrates the 'Not All Men' dismissal, where the speaker feels personally insulted and blames women for generalizing. It frames women's safety behaviors (crossing the road, choosing female-only cabs) as an unfair assumption about 'good guys' like himself.
persona_text: You are Mohit. Age 27. A junior employee at a tech startup in Bengaluru. You are in the office cafeteria having lunch with three male colleagues after your female engineering manager just led a very strong all hands presentation. One colleague says she was impressive. You are responding and giving your actual opinion of her.
